questions: Step 6: Should `dismissFinalVideo` be kept at all? It's currently called when switching to final video (TimelineEditor:644), but if we're removing the auto-detection badge, dismissal may be pointless — the switch itself is the action. Keeping it is safe but may be dead logic.

Step 4/5: Should 'Unpin' remain in the context menu, or should removal of a shot group use a different UX (e.g., a close button on the group bar)? The brief says to simplify but doesn't explicitly remove Unpin.

The floating final-video badge button (TimelineCanvas ~line 824) appears on groups with `hasFinalVideo`. Should it still appear on pinned groups, or was it only meaningful for inferred groups? If kept, it needs `finalVideoMap` wired through.

success_criteria.criterion: useShotGroups returns only pinned groups — no inference, no gap detection, no image-order checking
ShotGroup interface has no isPinned field; all groups are implicitly pinned
finalVideoShotIds prop and resolveFinalVideoShotIds function are removed from TimelineEditor
TimelineCanvas renders all groups with solid border-2 styling (no dashed/suggested distinction)
All shot group name labels always visible on hover (not conditional on isPinned)
Context menu: no 'Pin as Shot Group' action (all groups are already pinned)
Context menu: 'Unpin', 'Switch to Final Video', 'Switch to Images', 'Jump to Shot', 'Generate Video' still work on pinned groups
useShotGroups.test.ts passes with updated tests covering pinned-only behavior
No console.log debug statements remain in useShotGroups
useShotGroups hook signature is simplified — meta and registry parameters removed since they were only for inference
Module-level dismissedFinalVideoIds Set removed from useFinalVideoAvailable.ts
useShotGroups.ts is under 60 lines after cleanup
Manual smoke test: pinned groups display correctly, context menu actions work, final video switch works

assumptions: 'Unpin' stays in the context menu — removing pinned groups is still a valid user action.

The floating final-video badge button on groups should still render for pinned groups that have a final video available. We wire hasFinalVideo from finalVideoMap instead of the removed finalVideoShotIds.
dismissFinalVideo is kept since it's called during switch-to-final-video flow, even though its original purpose (suppressing auto-detected badges) is gone. Can be cleaned up in a follow-up.

The 'Dismiss reminder' context menu action can be removed since it was for suppressing the auto-detection badge on inferred groups. Pinned groups don't need dismissal — the user explicitly controls them.
